item.title: /qtddtot/ - Questions that don't deserve their own threads — 6 replies — 14.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
/qtddtot/ - Questions that don't deserve their own threads

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-04-12 21:21 UTC

OP

Hey /biz/
Been lurking for a bit. Looking to buy BTC for a while now so I can just sit on it for a few decades. I'm super cautious of any exchange apps -- I want to own my shit.

What's the easiest and safest way to buy BTC so that you'll have full legal ownership over it? Also, when would be ideal to buy? Should I wait a month? A year?

Also general questions thread. Precious metals, crypto, whatever the fuck

Early replies (first 3)

62108269
Question: Could everyone here buy this meme coin with like 6 holders and turn it into a /biz/coin? Let's finally get in early. Shrimple as

62108287
>>62108224
more like newfag spoonfeed thread

even if I could magically tell you to buy at some exact picobottom in the safest way possible, if you don't do your research properly you'll eventually get fucked like 99% of the market. there are many exchanges and many wallets and many security practices and everyone tell you something different, some will tell you should dollar cost average others to buy all at once others to never buy etc. it all comes down to doing your own research

62108342
you have to buy it on an exchange and withdraw it to an address you own. even cash app lets you withdraw bitcoin afaik.
Best is to get a hardware wallet like a trezor safe 3 or a ledger nano s plus.
Self custody these days means that you will create a 24 word seed that generates all your addresses. You will have to keep that secure. The point of a hardware wallet is that you also never have to enter those 24 words in a smartphone or computer. Only malware or scammers will try to get you to enter it on your phone/computer or a website.
anyway, do your own research. For longterm holding you might want multisig or to split your seed words (shamir secret sharing). Most serious bitcoiners use hardware wallets with electrum or sparrowwallet connected to their own node/electrum server.
I use 2-of-2 multisig with trezor and ledger hardware wallets, managed by sparrowwallet connected to my own node.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62108350
>>62108269
we all made money on PEPE

62108373
There’s no magic bullet, Anon.

Do the reasonable thing and accept that there is no such thing as get-rich-quick.
There’s no reasonable person who can hope to make a living off crypto, stocks, options or bonds unless you have a portfolio with a healthy $100k balance.

Follow intrinsic value, build your capital, have some discipline, and hope to retire in your 70s with a moderately good passive income from dividends on Blue Chip stocks or bonds.

As for crypto; you’re gambling.
Whatever made crypto better than Fiat was the fact that has been dismantled on the fact that it’s so mainstream and is now tracked to trim some bitcoin edges and pay off the hook-nose trolls.

The bubble is gone, we missed it.

Lick your wounds.

Hug, kiss, make love to the idea that investment is a slow, painful, boring process.

Precious metals are endgame loot.

It’s the edge against hyperinflation.

You’re far from the end zone.

Good luck.

62108387
>>62108350
Let's do it again

>and then scrambling to make an EVM to attract more developers
Actually, even this is not completely really true to be honest. The whole point of DOT 1.0 was to be able to make L2s. That was done and complete and there's like 40 L2s running on DOT. Then DOT 2.0 came and with it came Asset hub. Asset hub is a COMMON GOOD L2 that can run, as you've mentioned, EVM smart contracts and also PVM smart contracts which are SUPERIOR to EVM because the use the RISC-V architecture. You literally make it seem like launching EVM compatibility is a bad choice where in reality it's a move forward in order to make the chain more flexible.

https://polkadot.polkassembly.io/referenda/1864 Deploy Universal Deterministic Deployment Proxy (CREATE2 Factory) on Asset Hub
This ref passed today making it so that people can launch their EVM smart contracts on the same address as they have on ETH or other L2s. So Uniswap will soon launch on Asset Hub on the same address as they're on ETH or any other L2.

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-04-12 20:45 UTC

OP

I love watching normie carhag/carfag compilations where Normies complain about how everything is too expensive.

I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
>$820 rent utilities included
>free transportation (fare fags aren’t checking train during my commute hours)
>$300/mo groceries (steak and potatoes mostly)
>$60/mo unlimited data
>$15/mo rent insurance
>$30 dental insurance *shrugs*

I regularly spend $8 on coffee, $15 on cigs, $30 dollars on pizza, and I STILL usually dump minimum $1500 a month into my brokerage account. Next month my credit limit will go from 12k>24k when I open another chase CC. I have 6 months expenses in a savings account and the equivalent in dry powder in my brokerage.

Being completely unstressed/concerned about money and whipping out my girthy 12k CL sapphire preferred card on cutie coffee sluts 4 times a week to order my $9 dollar 24oz drum of double chocolate mocha and then ripping a Newport 100 on a comfy bench while i enjoy my coffee is all the pleasure I need in this world

Early replies (first 3)

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!
